mnemonics: LLF: Log of Likelihood Function sums up how likely your model's parameters are, given the observed data.
keyPoints: The Likelihood Function (LF) measures the probability of observing the data given a set of model parameters.
The Sum-of-Squares (SS) Function is often proportional to the negative log-likelihood for models with normally distributed errors.
Maximizing the Likelihood Function is equivalent to minimizing the Sum-of-Squares function for these common models.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Likelihood of the parameters with the probability of the data. Likelihood is about the parameters, not the data.
Assuming the Sum-of-Squares and Likelihood functions are always equivalent; this is only true under specific error distribution assumptions (e.g., normality).
Forgetting to take the logarithm of the Likelihood Function before maximization, which simplifies the calculation but changes the scale, not the optimum point.
